title: Custom Button Settings: Verify available provider options update when headset is disconnected
steps: 1. Launch the application with the headset connected to the PC.
2. Navigate to Custom Button settings page.
3. Observe and record the list of available provider options (e.g., MS Teams, Zoom, etc.).
4. Disconnect the headset from the PC.
5. Return to the Custom Button settings page without restarting the app.
6. Observe the list of available provider options again.
expected: The list of available provider options in Custom Button settings should update to reflect the disconnected state. Options that depend on the headset connection (e.g., MS Teams) should be removed, grayed out, or show a 'disconnected' status. The user should NOT need to restart the app to see the updated options.

title: Custom Button Settings: Verify provider options restore when headset is reconnected
steps: 1. Launch the application with the headset disconnected from the PC.
2. Navigate to Custom Button settings page.
3. Observe and record the list of available provider options (e.g., MS Teams should be missing or grayed out).
4. Reconnect the headset to the PC.
5. Return to the Custom Button settings page without restarting the app.
6. Observe the list of available provider options again.
expected: The list of available provider options in Custom Button settings should update to reflect the reconnected state. Options that were previously unavailable (e.g., MS Teams) should now appear or become selectable. The user should NOT need to restart the app to see the restored options.

title: Custom Button Settings: Verify no stale/cached options remain after headset disconnection
steps: 1. Launch the application with the headset connected to the PC.
2. Navigate to Custom Button settings and assign a custom action to a button using MS Teams as the provider.
3. Disconnect the headset from the PC.
4. Navigate back to Custom Button settings without restarting the app.
5. Check if the previously assigned MS Teams action is still listed as an available option or if it has been properly removed/disabled.
6. Attempt to modify the custom button assignment.
expected: After disconnecting the headset, the MS Teams provider option should no longer be available or should be clearly marked as disconnected. The previously assigned action should not remain as a selectable/active option if the provider is unavailable. The UI should reflect the current connection state accurately without stale cached data.

title: Custom Button Settings: Verify app restart is NOT required to reflect headset disconnection
steps: 1. Launch the application with the headset connected to the PC.
2. Navigate to Custom Button settings and note the available providers.
3. Disconnect the headset from the PC.
4. Immediately navigate back to Custom Button settings (do NOT restart the app).
5. Observe the available provider options.
6. Restart the application.
7. Navigate to Custom Button settings again and observe the available provider options.
expected: The available provider options observed in step 4 (without restart) should match the options observed in step 7 (after restart). No restart should be required to see the correct disconnected state. If the options differ, this confirms the bug that a restart is incorrectly needed.

title: Custom Button Settings: Verify UI state synchronization when headset is disconnected during settings interaction
steps: 1. Launch the application with the headset connected to the PC.
2. Navigate to Custom Button settings page.
3. While on the Custom Button settings page, disconnect the headset from the PC.
4. Wait for 2-3 seconds for any UI refresh/update mechanism to trigger.
5. Observe the available provider options on the current page without navigating away.
6. Navigate away from and back to the Custom Button settings page.
7. Observe the available provider options again.
expected: The available provider options should update dynamically when the headset is disconnected, even if the user is currently on the Custom Button settings page. If the options do not update in real-time (step 5), they should at least update when the user navigates away and back (step 7). In no case should the user be required to restart the app to see the correct state.
